item.content:encoded:
Ubuntu is one of the most widely used Linux distributions offering a fancy graphical user interface that Windows and macOS users are sure to enjoy. On the other hand, Google Chrome is one of the most widely used web browsers. So, if you want to use Google Chrome on an Ubuntu laptop or any other Ubuntu machine in place of the default and pre-installed Firefox web browser, you can. There are two ways you can install it. You can enjoy a manual download with the default Firefox web browser, similar to how you might use Edge to do this on Windows, or you can install it using the Terminal.

How to install Chrome on Ubuntu through the web

  1. Open Firefox
  2. Head to the Google Chrome download website.
  3. Choose the first option 64 bit .deb for Debian/Ubuntu.
  4. Choose Accept and Install.
  5. Save the file.
  6. When the download is complete, head to your Files app and click Downloads.
  7. Double-click to launch google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb. If you're having issues, right-click the right and choose Open with Software Center.
  8. The Ubuntu Software Center will open. Choose the green Install button.
  9. Enter your password.

You'll see a notification prompt about the installation being complete. You can access it from your applications list, and sign into the browser, sync your favorites and other history across your different operating systems or devices.

How to install Chrome on Ubuntu through the terminal

If you're the more technical type, you also can install Google Chrome using the terminal. This isn't quite easy, as you'll have to manually type out some commands, but in the end, it does the same thing as installing manually through a download. You'll fetch the file, then run a command to download it.

  1. Launch the Terminal app
  2. Type the following command: wget https://dl.google.com/linux/direct/google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb
  3. Type this command: sudo dpkg -i google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb
  4. Enter your password

After that, you'll see that Google Chrome will be available for you in your applications menu. You'll be able to sign in to your Google account and use the browser just like you would on any other platform. We suggest adding the app to your favorites, too. Just find it in the list of your applications and right-click it then choose Add to Favorites.

item.title: Best 13-inch laptops in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best 13-inch laptops in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Laptops are incredibly useful devices, and buying a new one can be very exciting, but also daunting. There's so much choice nowadays that finding the right laptop for you can be tough, but we're here to help. We already have a round-up of the best laptops overall, but what if you specifically want something that's lightweight and easy to carry around? Well, you can check out our list of the best lightweight laptops, or we can take a look at the best 13-inch laptops you can buy today.

While some users prefer bigger screens, 13-inch laptops are some of the most popular out there. Laptops are meant to be portable, and with this compact size, you can have something that's easy to take with you to school or work. Because it's such a popular segment, there are tons of great 13-inch laptops, and we've rounded up what we consider to be the best ones you can buy today.

  • HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
    Best overall
    $900 at HP
  • MacBook Air (M2)
    Best premium laptop
    $1087 at Amazon (13 inches)
  • Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
    Best clamshell laptop
    $1249 at Dell
  • Microsoft Surface Laptop 5
    Best premium Windows laptop
    $871 at Amazon
  • ASUS ROG Flow X13 (2023)
    Best gaming laptop
    $2500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070)
  • HP Dragonfly G4
    Best business laptop
    $1274 at HP
  • Microsoft Surface Pro 9
    Best tablet
    $954 at Amazon
  • HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook
    Best Chromebook
    $1099 at Newegg
  • Lenovo Yoga 6 Gen 8
    Best budget
    $555 at Lenovo
HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best overall

Performance, versatility, and a great screen

$900 $1500 Save $600

The Spectre x360 13.5 is one of the best convertibles HP has ever made, featuring a stunning premium design and a tall 3:2 display, all paired with high-end performance courtesy of 13th-gen Intel Core processors.

Pros
  • Fantastic 3:2 display including an OLED configuration option
  • 5MP webcam is great for video calls and meetings
  • Being a convertible makes it more versatile
Cons
  • Current configurations are expensive
  • A little heavy for its size
$900 at HP $1500 at Best Buy $1750 at Best Buy (OLED)

The HP Spectre x360 lineup is home to some of the best laptops on the market, and it's no surprise that the 2023 Spectre x360 13.5 makes the top of this list. It's a phenomenal laptop in almost every way, and the convertible form factor gives it some extra versatility.

Powering the HP Spectre x360 13.5 are 13th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1355U with 10 cores and 12 threads, which is all you need for great performance in daily tasks. The configurations available at writing time max out with 16GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, though you should see more options show up in the future. Past models have gone up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

Part of what makes this such a great laptop is the display, which is a 13.5-inch panel with a 3:2 aspect ratio. The base model comes with a Full HD+ IPS panel, which is already solid for the size. If you want an even better experience, there's also a 3K2K OLED model, which is sharper and has benefits like true blacks and more vibrant colors. Since it's a convertible, all models support touch and pen input. Every model also includes a 5MP webcam with Windows Hello support, one of the best you can find on any laptop.

HP hasn't changed the design of the Spectre x360 13.5 for 2023, so it's still a premium aluminum laptop with great build quality. It has a dual-tone design in both Nightfall Black or Nocturne Blue colorways, though there's also a simpler Natural Silver option. It weighs 3.01 pounds, which makes sense for a convertible of this size made of aluminum. For ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a microSD card reader, and a headphone jack.

Whether it's the performance, display, or design, there's a lot to love with the HP Spectre x360 13.5, and it's an easy recommendation for almost anyone. You can read our review of last year's model, which is very similar in many ways.

MacBook Air (M2)
Best premium laptop

Premium build and terrific performance

The latest MacBook Air is deceivingly lightweight and compact. Thanks to the Apple M2 processor, it's a surprisingly fast laptop with fantastic battery life, and it also has a great display with sharp resolution and accurate colors. Plus, it comes in four color options.

Pros
  • The Apple M2 processor offers fantastic performance and battery life
  • Sharp Retina display is included by default
  • 1080p webcam
  • One of the thinnest and most portable laptops
Cons
  • Only one external display is natively supported
  • Upgrades can get very expensive
  • The base storage configuration is noticeably slower
$1087 at Amazon (13 inches) $1099 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1099 at Adorama

No list of 13-inch laptops could go without mentioning the MacBook Air, and that's especially true with the latest model, which delivers some very welcome improvements in performance, design, and the webcam.

Focusing on performance first, the latest MacBook Air is powered by the Apple M2 with eight CPU cores, and it promises 18% more performance than the Apple M1. Plus, it also has an upgraded 10-core GPU (in some configurations), which delivers up to 35% more performance than the previous generation. This makes it one of the fastest lightweight laptops around, and it's also very efficient, so battery life is phenomenal. You can also configure the latest MacBook Air with up to 24GB of unified memory (RAM) and a 2TB SSD.

The display has also changed a bit in the latest MacBook Air. It's slightly taller and measures 13.6 inches diagonally, and the resolution is 2560x1664, so it's super sharp. It also now reaches up to 500 nits of brightness instead of 400 nits, so it's a bit more visible outdoors, and it supports P3 Wide Color. A potential downside is a notch that houses the webcam, but at least that webcam is now a 1080p sensor, so it has pretty good quality. It lacks Face ID support, though, so you still have to use Touch ID.

Design-wise, the latest MacBook Air is very different from its predecessors, ditching the wedge-shaped design for a flat, even look with rounded edges. It's still incredibly thin at just over 11mm, and it also weighs just 2.7 pounds. Plus, it comes in four colors, including the classic Silver and Space Grey, along with newcomers Starlight and Midnight. As for ports, you get two Thunderbolt/USB4 ports and a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, along with MagSafe 3 support for fast charging, a major addition to this model. It's worth noting that the laptop only supports one external display, however.

While it has some shortcomings, the latest MacBook Air is easily one of the best 13-inch laptops on the market today.

Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
Best clamshell laptop

Ultra-modern look with a fantastic display

$1249 $1499 Save $250

The Dell XPS 13 Plus is a powerful laptop with an ultra-modern design, featuring an invisible touchpad. It has powerful 13th-generation Intel processors and stunning display options, including a 3.5K OLED panel.

Pros
  • Powerful 13th-gen Intel P-series processors
  • Modern and futuristic design
  • Colorful and vibrant display, including an OLED model
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Only two Thunderbolt ports
  • Seamless trackpad and touch buttons may take some getting used to
$1249 at Dell $1950 at Best Buy $1692 at Amazon

Dell is another company well known for making some of the best laptops on the market, and the latest iteration of the XPS 13 Plus is yet another easy choice if you want a 13-inch laptop.

The latest model comes with 13th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically a Core i7-1360P with 12 cores and 16 threads, which gives it more than enough performance to handle all kinds of day-to-day tasks. This is a very fast processor, and it's combined with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

It's got a great display, too, being a 13.4-inch panel with a tall 16:10 aspect ratio. The base model comes in Full HD+ resolution, which is already great, but you can upgrade to a stunning 3.5K OLED option that's very sharp while also having the usual benefits of OLED, like true blacks, vivid colors, and high contrast ratios. There's also an Ultra HD+ LCD option, which is ideal for creative work since it covers 100% of Adobe RGB. The webcam is a big downside since Dell insists on using a 720p sensor. However, it does support Windows Hello, so there's a positive there.

A few things make this laptop a unique and appealing one in terms of design, too. It has an invisible touchpad that blends seamlessly with the chassis, and the zero-lattice keyboard combined with the touch-based function row all make for an incredibly modern-feeling laptop, though it may take some getting used to. It's also fairly light, at 2.71 pounds. A downside of this machine, however, is the ports, which are limited to just two Thunderbolt 4 connections, with adapters in the box for a headphone jack and USB Type-A.

The XPS 13 Plus certainly has its downsides, but it's still an incredible machine in more ways than one.

Microsoft Surface Laptop 5
Best premium Windows laptop

Sleek design and sharp display

$871 $999 Save $128

The Surface Laptop 5 is a premium laptop with top-tier specs and a great display for getting work done. It comes in an all-metal chassis that has four colors to choose from.

Pros
  • Tall 3:2 display is great for productivity
  • Premium build quality with an aluminum chassis and optional Alcantara fabric
  • Decent port selection including Thunderbolt 4
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • This design is a few years old at this point
$900 at Best Buy (13 inches) $871 at Amazon $871 at B&H

The Surface Laptop is kind of the Windows equivalent to the MacBook Air, and if you're looking for a premium laptop that runs Windows, then the Surface Laptop 5 is a safe bet.

For starters, the Surface Laptop 5 comes with 12th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically up to an Intel Core i7-1255U, with 10 cores and 12 threads. It's a very capable processor. It will handle day-to-day tasks just fine, though it's not the newest hardware around anymore. The 13-inch version of the Surface Laptop 5 goes up to 16GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD, so you have a great experience with this machine.

One of the highlights of the Surface Laptop 5 is definitely the display. Microsoft uses its signature 3:2 aspect ratio and a very sharp resolution of 2256x1504, so you'll have a great experience with it in every way. The screen also supports touch and pen input, though this isn't a convertible. A big downside to this laptop is the 720p webcam that sits at the top of the display, though it does support Windows Hello facial recognition.

Premium design is also a trademark of Surface devices, and the Surface Laptop 5 is no exception. You get an all-metal design in four color options, those being Platinum, Matte Black, Sandstone, and Sage. The Platinum model also comes with an Alcantara fabric cover for the keyboard base, making it a bit more comfortable. At 2.8 pounds, it's a fairly portable laptop, too. Ports include one Thunderbolt 4 port, one USB Type-A port, a headphone jack, and a Surface Connect port for charging. This is the first Surface laptop model with Thunderbolt support, which is a major addition.

The older processors and 720p webcam are notable downsides, but the Surface Laptop 5 is still a great premium laptop for most users. However, the Surface Laptop 6 could be right around the corner, so it may be worth waiting.

ASUS ROG Flow X13 (2023)
Best gaming laptop

Powerful performance in a lightweight package

The Asus ROG Flow X13 may be small, but it packs a punch. With an AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS and powerful Nvidia graphics, you can use this lightweight laptop for gaming while getting the versatility of a convertible. It has a beautiful display, too.

Pros
  • Powerful AMD processors and Nvidia graphics
  • Super lightweight and portable considering the performance
  • Beautiful Quad HD+ 165Hz display
Cons
  • Much more expensive than previous models
  • Battery life won't be great
$2500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070) $2500 at Asus $2900 at Newegg

Gaming usually requires pretty beefy hardware, but Asus managed to squeeze some gaming power into a portable machine with the ROG Flow X13, one of my favorite laptops ever.

The 2023 iteration of the ROG Flow X13 comes with an AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS processor with 8 cores and 16 threads, and that's paired with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU, delivering very impressive performance for such a small laptop. It also comes with up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD. If you want more graphics power, you can also hook up an Asus ROG Xg Mobile external GPU, with options up to an RTX 4090.

The display on the ROG Flow X13 is a 13.4-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, with options up to a stunning ROG Nebula display with Quad HD+ resolution, 100% coverage of DCI-P3, and a 165Hz refresh rate. It's fantastic for gaming and everything else. It supports touch and pen input, too. Above that display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support.

On to the design, the Asus ROG Flow X13 is very sleek with an all-black chassis that's just 18.7mm thick at its thickest point and a mere 2.87 pounds of weight, very impressive for these specs. It also has a solid supply of ports, with two USB Type-C ports (one integrated into the ROG Xg Mobile interface), USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. That's very good for this size.

This is a very impressive 13-inch laptop, and if you're interested in gaming while still having a lightweight design, it's one of your best options.

HP Dragonfly G4
Best business laptop

Sleek, lightweight, and performant

$1274 $2548 Save $1274

With top-of-the-line Intel processors, a sleek design, and a great webcam, the HP Dragonfly G4 is perfect for professionals, whether they're working from home or in the office. The lightweight chassis makes it easy to carry around, too, so it's great for travel.

Pros
  • Sleek and lightweight design makes it great for working from anywhere
  • Fast 13th-gen Intel processors
  • One of the best webcams on a laptop
Cons
  • Expensive, outside of sales
  • The non-OLED display isn't amazing
$1274 at HP $1769 at B&H $1752 at Newegg

Professional users need a reliable laptop that looks good in a business environment and is easy to carry around. The HP Dragonfly G4 ticks all these boxes, with some extra perks, too.

Inside the HP Dragonfly G4 are 13th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1365U with vPro, featuring 10 cores and 12 threads for great performance while having solid battery life. It also comes with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

You also get a 13.5-inch panel with a tall 3:2 aspect ratio here, and the base model comes in an average Full HD+ resolution with 400 nits of brightness. However, there are upgrade options including one with HP Sure View Reflect and 1000 nits of brightness, or a beautiful 3K2K OLED panel with vibrant colors. You even get a 5MP webcam, one of the best you'll find on any laptop, complete with Windows Hello support.

As for the design, it's as sleek and subdued as you could want. It comes in either Slate Blue or Natural Silver (though the former is more interesting), and it weighs just 2.2 pounds, meaning it's super portable and easy to carry. Plus, it has great connectivity options, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and an optional nano-SIM slot if you get the LTE or 5G models. And yes, cellular connectivity is an option if you want to work on the move.

Outside of discounts, the HP Dragonfly G4 is pretty pricy, but it's a fantastic laptop for almost everyone, and especially for business.

Microsoft Surface Pro 9
Best tablet

Extra portable and versatile

$954 $1000 Save $46

The Surface Pro 9 is arguably the best Windows tablet on the market, with top-tier performance and a fantastic display with a very sharp resolution and 120Hz refresh rate. It also comes in colors for the first time ever.

Pros
  • Very sharp 3:2 display with a smooth 120Hz refresh rate
  • Some of the best cameras on any Windows PC
  • Intel and Arm-based options, with the latter including 5G
Cons
  • Intel model no longer has the newest processors
  • Arm model doesn't support Thunderbolt
$1000 at Best Buy (Wi-Fi model) $1300 at Best Buy (5G model) $954 at Amazon

There aren't a lot of premium Windows tablets out there, but that shouldn't take away from how great the Surface Pro 9 is. With a great display and cameras, plus other high-end specs, this is a fantastic device.

On the inside, the Surface Pro 9 features 12th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1255U. That gets you 10 cores and 12 threads, so it's plenty fast. You can also configure it with up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, which is fantastic for a tablet. There's also the Surface Pro 9 with 5G, which is powered by an Arm-based Microsoft SQ3 chipset, along with up to 16GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD. It's not as fast, but it's still great.

The display on the Surface Pro 9 is one of its best qualities. It has a 13-inch display with a 3:2 aspect ratio, and it comes in a very sharp 2880x1920 resolution and a smooth 120Hz refresh rate, which can adjust dynamically. Being a tablet also means the Surface Pro 9 has one of the best webcams around. The front-facing camera is a 5MP sensor that supports 1080p video, in addition to Windows Hello facial recognition. There's also a 10MP camera on the back with 4K video support.

Microsoft didn't change much about the design of the Surface Pro 9, so it still weighs around 1.94 pounds depending on the configuration, and it's also 9.4mm thin, excluding the optional keyboard. This model does add new color options in the Intel version, with Forest and Sapphire joining the Platinum and Graphite colorways. Unfortunately, in terms of ports, you only get two USB-C ports (supporting Thunderbolt 4, on the Intel model) and Surface Connect, meaning there's not even a headphone jack.

Despite that, the Surface Pro 9 is easily the best Windows tablet on the market, though we're expecting a Surface Pro 10 to launch very soon.

HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook
Best Chromebook

A premium ChromeOS convertible

The HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is possibly the most premium Chromebook on the market right now. It has a premium convertible design and high-end 12th-gen Intel processors, plus a fantastic display.

Pros
  • Fast 12th-gen Intel processors
  • Sharp 5MP webcam
  • Sharp and bright display
Cons
  • It's very expensive
  • No OLED display option like the Windows counterpart
$2177 at HP $1099 at Newegg

Not many companies are willing to put their best efforts into making a premium Chromebook, but HP has challenged that trend with the Elite Dragonfly Chromebook. This is one of the most premium Chromebooks around, despite being a year old.

For starters, in terms of performance, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook packs Intel's 12th-generation Core processors, up to an Intel Core i7-1265U, which has 10 cores and 12 threads and can run Chrome OS like a dream. Plus, you can get up to 32GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD, which is massive for ChromeOS.

The display on the Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is a 13.5-inch panel with a 3:2 aspect ratio. It comes in Full HD+ resolution in the base model, with the option to add HP Sure View Reflect, a privacy screen that prevents people around you from seeing what's on your screen. You can also upgrade to a "Quad HD+" model (2256x1504), and all models include a stylus, since the touchscreen also supports pen input. Above the display, you get a great 5MP webcam, but instead of facial recognition, this one has a fingerprint reader.

Design-wise, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is a fairly standard business laptop, coming in a subtle Slate Blue colorway that's very close to black. The laptop weighs 2.8 pounds, so it's fairly portable, especially considering it's a convertible. As for ports, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook has two Thunderbolt 4 ports (though external GPUs won't work with Chrome OS), USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader.

The biggest downside of the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is its exorbitant price tag, but thankfully it gets discounted often. Otherwise, this is definitely one of the best 13-inch laptops on the market.

Lenovo Yoga 6 Gen 8
Best budget

A versatile laptop on the cheap

$555 $700 Save $145

The Lenovo Yoga 6 Gen 8 is an excellent budget Windows convertible, featuring Ryzen 7030 series processors and other solid specs, including a 1080p webcam and Windows Hello support.

Pros
  • Modern AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors
  • 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support
  • Unique design with optional fabric cover
Cons
  • Performance isn't on the same level as more expensive models
$700 at Best Buy $555 at Lenovo $555 at Newegg

Not everyone can afford to spend over $1,000 on a laptop, but thankfully, the Lenovo Yoga 6 to give you an excellent laptop for a very reasonable price. Starting at just $700, it's a no-brainer.

For that price, you're getting an AMD Ryzen 5 7530U, with 6 cores and 12 threads, delivering solid performance for all kinds of day-to-day tasks, whether that's browsing the web or writing up documents. It also has 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD, though you can upgrade both if you have the budget for it.

It also comes with a 13.3-inch display that has a 16:10 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution. That will give you a pretty great experience for the price, and the 300 nits of brightness are about what you might expect. It also supports touch and pen input, since it's a convertible. The webcam is a 1080p sensor, which is great to see in a budget laptop, and it even supports Windows Hello facial recognition.

Lenovo also has a fairly unique design here, with the top half of the laptop made of metal and the bottom half made of plastic. Some models also have a fabric cover on the top half, which makes it even more unique. Starting at 3.02 pounds, the laptop is also reasonably light. It even gets ports right, featuring two USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack to cover all your needs. There's no USB4 support, but that makes sense for a budget laptop.

Considering it's a convertible and everything else it offers, it's a very good deal. I reviewed the previous model, which was nearly identical aside from the processors, and praised it heavily.

Best 13-inch laptops in 2023: Bottom line

And those are the best 13-inch laptops you can buy today. We chose the HP Spectre x360 13.5 for the top spot because it has a fantastic combination of high-end performance, great display options, and a beautiful dual-tone design, plus the versatility of a convertible. It's a laptop you can't really go wrong with, so it deserves the top spot, but all the other laptops on this list are also great choices. A personal favorite of mine is the Asus ROG Flow X13 since it has the versatility of a convertible, but also some powerful internals that allow for some gaming.

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best overall
$900 $1500 Save $600

The Spectre x360 13.5 is one of the best convertibles HP has ever made, featuring a stunning premium design and a tall 3:2 display, all paired with high-end performance.

$900 at HP $1500 at Best Buy $1750 at Best Buy (OLED)

Of course, if you want something cheaper, the Lenovo Yoga 6 is the one for you. This is a personal favorite of mine, and it's simply a great laptop for the price, with solid performance, display, and design. You can't go wrong with this one if you're on a tighter budget.

If you're more interested in bigger laptops, we also have a list of the best 15-inch laptops you can buy right now. This larger size also makes room for more power, so you can get much more powerful gaming laptops.
item.description:
Laptops are incredibly useful devices, and buying a new one can be very exciting, but also daunting. There's so much choice nowadays that finding the right laptop for you can be tough, but we're here to help. We already have a round-up of the best laptops overall, but what if you specifically want something that's lightweight and easy to carry around? Well, you can check out our list of the best lightweight laptops, or we can take a look at the best 13-inch laptops you can buy today.

While some users prefer bigger screens, 13-inch laptops are some of the most popular out there. Laptops are meant to be portable, and with this compact size, you can have something that's easy to take with you to school or work. Because it's such a popular segment, there are tons of great 13-inch laptops, and we've rounded up what we consider to be the best ones you can buy today.

  • HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
    Best overall
    $900 at HP
  • MacBook Air (M2)
    Best premium laptop
    $1087 at Amazon (13 inches)
  • Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
    Best clamshell laptop
    $1249 at Dell
  • Microsoft Surface Laptop 5
    Best premium Windows laptop
    $871 at Amazon
  • ASUS ROG Flow X13 (2023)
    Best gaming laptop
    $2500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070)
  • HP Dragonfly G4
    Best business laptop
    $1274 at HP
  • Microsoft Surface Pro 9
    Best tablet
    $954 at Amazon
  • HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook
    Best Chromebook
    $1099 at Newegg
  • Lenovo Yoga 6 Gen 8
    Best budget
    $555 at Lenovo
HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best overall

Performance, versatility, and a great screen

$900 $1500 Save $600

The Spectre x360 13.5 is one of the best convertibles HP has ever made, featuring a stunning premium design and a tall 3:2 display, all paired with high-end performance courtesy of 13th-gen Intel Core processors.

Pros
  • Fantastic 3:2 display including an OLED configuration option
  • 5MP webcam is great for video calls and meetings
  • Being a convertible makes it more versatile
Cons
  • Current configurations are expensive
  • A little heavy for its size
$900 at HP $1500 at Best Buy $1750 at Best Buy (OLED)

The HP Spectre x360 lineup is home to some of the best laptops on the market, and it's no surprise that the 2023 Spectre x360 13.5 makes the top of this list. It's a phenomenal laptop in almost every way, and the convertible form factor gives it some extra versatility.

Powering the HP Spectre x360 13.5 are 13th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1355U with 10 cores and 12 threads, which is all you need for great performance in daily tasks. The configurations available at writing time max out with 16GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, though you should see more options show up in the future. Past models have gone up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

Part of what makes this such a great laptop is the display, which is a 13.5-inch panel with a 3:2 aspect ratio. The base model comes with a Full HD+ IPS panel, which is already solid for the size. If you want an even better experience, there's also a 3K2K OLED model, which is sharper and has benefits like true blacks and more vibrant colors. Since it's a convertible, all models support touch and pen input. Every model also includes a 5MP webcam with Windows Hello support, one of the best you can find on any laptop.

HP hasn't changed the design of the Spectre x360 13.5 for 2023, so it's still a premium aluminum laptop with great build quality. It has a dual-tone design in both Nightfall Black or Nocturne Blue colorways, though there's also a simpler Natural Silver option. It weighs 3.01 pounds, which makes sense for a convertible of this size made of aluminum. For ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a microSD card reader, and a headphone jack.

Whether it's the performance, display, or design, there's a lot to love with the HP Spectre x360 13.5, and it's an easy recommendation for almost anyone. You can read our review of last year's model, which is very similar in many ways.

MacBook Air (M2)
Best premium laptop

Premium build and terrific performance

The latest MacBook Air is deceivingly lightweight and compact. Thanks to the Apple M2 processor, it's a surprisingly fast laptop with fantastic battery life, and it also has a great display with sharp resolution and accurate colors. Plus, it comes in four color options.

Pros
  • The Apple M2 processor offers fantastic performance and battery life
  • Sharp Retina display is included by default
  • 1080p webcam
  • One of the thinnest and most portable laptops
Cons
  • Only one external display is natively supported
  • Upgrades can get very expensive
  • The base storage configuration is noticeably slower
$1087 at Amazon (13 inches) $1099 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1099 at Adorama

No list of 13-inch laptops could go without mentioning the MacBook Air, and that's especially true with the latest model, which delivers some very welcome improvements in performance, design, and the webcam.

Focusing on performance first, the latest MacBook Air is powered by the Apple M2 with eight CPU cores, and it promises 18% more performance than the Apple M1. Plus, it also has an upgraded 10-core GPU (in some configurations), which delivers up to 35% more performance than the previous generation. This makes it one of the fastest lightweight laptops around, and it's also very efficient, so battery life is phenomenal. You can also configure the latest MacBook Air with up to 24GB of unified memory (RAM) and a 2TB SSD.

The display has also changed a bit in the latest MacBook Air. It's slightly taller and measures 13.6 inches diagonally, and the resolution is 2560x1664, so it's super sharp. It also now reaches up to 500 nits of brightness instead of 400 nits, so it's a bit more visible outdoors, and it supports P3 Wide Color. A potential downside is a notch that houses the webcam, but at least that webcam is now a 1080p sensor, so it has pretty good quality. It lacks Face ID support, though, so you still have to use Touch ID.

Design-wise, the latest MacBook Air is very different from its predecessors, ditching the wedge-shaped design for a flat, even look with rounded edges. It's still incredibly thin at just over 11mm, and it also weighs just 2.7 pounds. Plus, it comes in four colors, including the classic Silver and Space Grey, along with newcomers Starlight and Midnight. As for ports, you get two Thunderbolt/USB4 ports and a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, along with MagSafe 3 support for fast charging, a major addition to this model. It's worth noting that the laptop only supports one external display, however.

While it has some shortcomings, the latest MacBook Air is easily one of the best 13-inch laptops on the market today.

Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
Best clamshell laptop

Ultra-modern look with a fantastic display

$1249 $1499 Save $250

The Dell XPS 13 Plus is a powerful laptop with an ultra-modern design, featuring an invisible touchpad. It has powerful 13th-generation Intel processors and stunning display options, including a 3.5K OLED panel.

Pros
  • Powerful 13th-gen Intel P-series processors
  • Modern and futuristic design
  • Colorful and vibrant display, including an OLED model
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Only two Thunderbolt ports
  • Seamless trackpad and touch buttons may take some getting used to
$1249 at Dell $1950 at Best Buy $1692 at Amazon

Dell is another company well known for making some of the best laptops on the market, and the latest iteration of the XPS 13 Plus is yet another easy choice if you want a 13-inch laptop.

The latest model comes with 13th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically a Core i7-1360P with 12 cores and 16 threads, which gives it more than enough performance to handle all kinds of day-to-day tasks. This is a very fast processor, and it's combined with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

It's got a great display, too, being a 13.4-inch panel with a tall 16:10 aspect ratio. The base model comes in Full HD+ resolution, which is already great, but you can upgrade to a stunning 3.5K OLED option that's very sharp while also having the usual benefits of OLED, like true blacks, vivid colors, and high contrast ratios. There's also an Ultra HD+ LCD option, which is ideal for creative work since it covers 100% of Adobe RGB. The webcam is a big downside since Dell insists on using a 720p sensor. However, it does support Windows Hello, so there's a positive there.

A few things make this laptop a unique and appealing one in terms of design, too. It has an invisible touchpad that blends seamlessly with the chassis, and the zero-lattice keyboard combined with the touch-based function row all make for an incredibly modern-feeling laptop, though it may take some getting used to. It's also fairly light, at 2.71 pounds. A downside of this machine, however, is the ports, which are limited to just two Thunderbolt 4 connections, with adapters in the box for a headphone jack and USB Type-A.

The XPS 13 Plus certainly has its downsides, but it's still an incredible machine in more ways than one.

Microsoft Surface Laptop 5
Best premium Windows laptop

Sleek design and sharp display

$871 $999 Save $128

The Surface Laptop 5 is a premium laptop with top-tier specs and a great display for getting work done. It comes in an all-metal chassis that has four colors to choose from.

Pros
  • Tall 3:2 display is great for productivity
  • Premium build quality with an aluminum chassis and optional Alcantara fabric
  • Decent port selection including Thunderbolt 4
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • This design is a few years old at this point
$900 at Best Buy (13 inches) $871 at Amazon $871 at B&H

The Surface Laptop is kind of the Windows equivalent to the MacBook Air, and if you're looking for a premium laptop that runs Windows, then the Surface Laptop 5 is a safe bet.

For starters, the Surface Laptop 5 comes with 12th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically up to an Intel Core i7-1255U, with 10 cores and 12 threads. It's a very capable processor. It will handle day-to-day tasks just fine, though it's not the newest hardware around anymore. The 13-inch version of the Surface Laptop 5 goes up to 16GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD, so you have a great experience with this machine.

One of the highlights of the Surface Laptop 5 is definitely the display. Microsoft uses its signature 3:2 aspect ratio and a very sharp resolution of 2256x1504, so you'll have a great experience with it in every way. The screen also supports touch and pen input, though this isn't a convertible. A big downside to this laptop is the 720p webcam that sits at the top of the display, though it does support Windows Hello facial recognition.

Premium design is also a trademark of Surface devices, and the Surface Laptop 5 is no exception. You get an all-metal design in four color options, those being Platinum, Matte Black, Sandstone, and Sage. The Platinum model also comes with an Alcantara fabric cover for the keyboard base, making it a bit more comfortable. At 2.8 pounds, it's a fairly portable laptop, too. Ports include one Thunderbolt 4 port, one USB Type-A port, a headphone jack, and a Surface Connect port for charging. This is the first Surface laptop model with Thunderbolt support, which is a major addition.

The older processors and 720p webcam are notable downsides, but the Surface Laptop 5 is still a great premium laptop for most users. However, the Surface Laptop 6 could be right around the corner, so it may be worth waiting.

ASUS ROG Flow X13 (2023)
Best gaming laptop

Powerful performance in a lightweight package

The Asus ROG Flow X13 may be small, but it packs a punch. With an AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS and powerful Nvidia graphics, you can use this lightweight laptop for gaming while getting the versatility of a convertible. It has a beautiful display, too.

Pros
  • Powerful AMD processors and Nvidia graphics
  • Super lightweight and portable considering the performance
  • Beautiful Quad HD+ 165Hz display
Cons
  • Much more expensive than previous models
  • Battery life won't be great
$2500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070) $2500 at Asus $2900 at Newegg

Gaming usually requires pretty beefy hardware, but Asus managed to squeeze some gaming power into a portable machine with the ROG Flow X13, one of my favorite laptops ever.

The 2023 iteration of the ROG Flow X13 comes with an AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS processor with 8 cores and 16 threads, and that's paired with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU, delivering very impressive performance for such a small laptop. It also comes with up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD. If you want more graphics power, you can also hook up an Asus ROG Xg Mobile external GPU, with options up to an RTX 4090.

The display on the ROG Flow X13 is a 13.4-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, with options up to a stunning ROG Nebula display with Quad HD+ resolution, 100% coverage of DCI-P3, and a 165Hz refresh rate. It's fantastic for gaming and everything else. It supports touch and pen input, too. Above that display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support.

On to the design, the Asus ROG Flow X13 is very sleek with an all-black chassis that's just 18.7mm thick at its thickest point and a mere 2.87 pounds of weight, very impressive for these specs. It also has a solid supply of ports, with two USB Type-C ports (one integrated into the ROG Xg Mobile interface), USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. That's very good for this size.

This is a very impressive 13-inch laptop, and if you're interested in gaming while still having a lightweight design, it's one of your best options.

HP Dragonfly G4
Best business laptop

Sleek, lightweight, and performant

$1274 $2548 Save $1274

With top-of-the-line Intel processors, a sleek design, and a great webcam, the HP Dragonfly G4 is perfect for professionals, whether they're working from home or in the office. The lightweight chassis makes it easy to carry around, too, so it's great for travel.

Pros
  • Sleek and lightweight design makes it great for working from anywhere
  • Fast 13th-gen Intel processors
  • One of the best webcams on a laptop
Cons
  • Expensive, outside of sales
  • The non-OLED display isn't amazing
$1274 at HP $1769 at B&H $1752 at Newegg

Professional users need a reliable laptop that looks good in a business environment and is easy to carry around. The HP Dragonfly G4 ticks all these boxes, with some extra perks, too.

Inside the HP Dragonfly G4 are 13th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1365U with vPro, featuring 10 cores and 12 threads for great performance while having solid battery life. It also comes with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

You also get a 13.5-inch panel with a tall 3:2 aspect ratio here, and the base model comes in an average Full HD+ resolution with 400 nits of brightness. However, there are upgrade options including one with HP Sure View Reflect and 1000 nits of brightness, or a beautiful 3K2K OLED panel with vibrant colors. You even get a 5MP webcam, one of the best you'll find on any laptop, complete with Windows Hello support.

As for the design, it's as sleek and subdued as you could want. It comes in either Slate Blue or Natural Silver (though the former is more interesting), and it weighs just 2.2 pounds, meaning it's super portable and easy to carry. Plus, it has great connectivity options, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and an optional nano-SIM slot if you get the LTE or 5G models. And yes, cellular connectivity is an option if you want to work on the move.

Outside of discounts, the HP Dragonfly G4 is pretty pricy, but it's a fantastic laptop for almost everyone, and especially for business.

Microsoft Surface Pro 9
Best tablet

Extra portable and versatile

$954 $1000 Save $46

The Surface Pro 9 is arguably the best Windows tablet on the market, with top-tier performance and a fantastic display with a very sharp resolution and 120Hz refresh rate. It also comes in colors for the first time ever.

Pros
  • Very sharp 3:2 display with a smooth 120Hz refresh rate
  • Some of the best cameras on any Windows PC
  • Intel and Arm-based options, with the latter including 5G
Cons
  • Intel model no longer has the newest processors
  • Arm model doesn't support Thunderbolt
$1000 at Best Buy (Wi-Fi model) $1300 at Best Buy (5G model) $954 at Amazon

There aren't a lot of premium Windows tablets out there, but that shouldn't take away from how great the Surface Pro 9 is. With a great display and cameras, plus other high-end specs, this is a fantastic device.

On the inside, the Surface Pro 9 features 12th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1255U. That gets you 10 cores and 12 threads, so it's plenty fast. You can also configure it with up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, which is fantastic for a tablet. There's also the Surface Pro 9 with 5G, which is powered by an Arm-based Microsoft SQ3 chipset, along with up to 16GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD. It's not as fast, but it's still great.

The display on the Surface Pro 9 is one of its best qualities. It has a 13-inch display with a 3:2 aspect ratio, and it comes in a very sharp 2880x1920 resolution and a smooth 120Hz refresh rate, which can adjust dynamically. Being a tablet also means the Surface Pro 9 has one of the best webcams around. The front-facing camera is a 5MP sensor that supports 1080p video, in addition to Windows Hello facial recognition. There's also a 10MP camera on the back with 4K video support.

Microsoft didn't change much about the design of the Surface Pro 9, so it still weighs around 1.94 pounds depending on the configuration, and it's also 9.4mm thin, excluding the optional keyboard. This model does add new color options in the Intel version, with Forest and Sapphire joining the Platinum and Graphite colorways. Unfortunately, in terms of ports, you only get two USB-C ports (supporting Thunderbolt 4, on the Intel model) and Surface Connect, meaning there's not even a headphone jack.

Despite that, the Surface Pro 9 is easily the best Windows tablet on the market, though we're expecting a Surface Pro 10 to launch very soon.

HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook
Best Chromebook

A premium ChromeOS convertible

The HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is possibly the most premium Chromebook on the market right now. It has a premium convertible design and high-end 12th-gen Intel processors, plus a fantastic display.

Pros
  • Fast 12th-gen Intel processors
  • Sharp 5MP webcam
  • Sharp and bright display
Cons
  • It's very expensive
  • No OLED display option like the Windows counterpart
$2177 at HP $1099 at Newegg

Not many companies are willing to put their best efforts into making a premium Chromebook, but HP has challenged that trend with the Elite Dragonfly Chromebook. This is one of the most premium Chromebooks around, despite being a year old.

For starters, in terms of performance, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook packs Intel's 12th-generation Core processors, up to an Intel Core i7-1265U, which has 10 cores and 12 threads and can run Chrome OS like a dream. Plus, you can get up to 32GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD, which is massive for ChromeOS.

The display on the Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is a 13.5-inch panel with a 3:2 aspect ratio. It comes in Full HD+ resolution in the base model, with the option to add HP Sure View Reflect, a privacy screen that prevents people around you from seeing what's on your screen. You can also upgrade to a "Quad HD+" model (2256x1504), and all models include a stylus, since the touchscreen also supports pen input. Above the display, you get a great 5MP webcam, but instead of facial recognition, this one has a fingerprint reader.

Design-wise, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is a fairly standard business laptop, coming in a subtle Slate Blue colorway that's very close to black. The laptop weighs 2.8 pounds, so it's fairly portable, especially considering it's a convertible. As for ports, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook has two Thunderbolt 4 ports (though external GPUs won't work with Chrome OS), USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader.

The biggest downside of the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is its exorbitant price tag, but thankfully it gets discounted often. Otherwise, this is definitely one of the best 13-inch laptops on the market.

Lenovo Yoga 6 Gen 8
Best budget

A versatile laptop on the cheap

$555 $700 Save $145

The Lenovo Yoga 6 Gen 8 is an excellent budget Windows convertible, featuring Ryzen 7030 series processors and other solid specs, including a 1080p webcam and Windows Hello support.

Pros
  • Modern AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors
  • 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support
  • Unique design with optional fabric cover
Cons
  • Performance isn't on the same level as more expensive models
$700 at Best Buy $555 at Lenovo $555 at Newegg

Not everyone can afford to spend over $1,000 on a laptop, but thankfully, the Lenovo Yoga 6 to give you an excellent laptop for a very reasonable price. Starting at just $700, it's a no-brainer.

For that price, you're getting an AMD Ryzen 5 7530U, with 6 cores and 12 threads, delivering solid performance for all kinds of day-to-day tasks, whether that's browsing the web or writing up documents. It also has 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD, though you can upgrade both if you have the budget for it.

It also comes with a 13.3-inch display that has a 16:10 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution. That will give you a pretty great experience for the price, and the 300 nits of brightness are about what you might expect. It also supports touch and pen input, since it's a convertible. Some models also have a fabric cover on the top half, which makes it even more unique. Starting at 3.02 pounds, the laptop is also reasonably light. It even gets ports right, featuring two USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack to cover all your needs. There's no USB4 support, but that makes sense for a budget laptop.

Considering it's a convertible and everything else it offers, it's a very good deal. I reviewed the previous model, which was nearly identical aside from the processors, and praised it heavily.

Best 13-inch laptops in 2023: Bottom line

And those are the best 13-inch laptops you can buy today. $900 $1500 Save $600

$900 at HP $1500 at Best Buy $1750 at Best Buy (OLED)

The HP Spectre x360 lineup is home to some of the best laptops on the market, and it's no surprise that the 2023 Spectre x360 13.5 makes the top of this list. It's a phenomenal laptop in almost every way, and the convertible form factor gives it some extra versatility.

Powering the HP Spectre x360 13.5 are 13th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1355U with 10 cores and 12 threads, which is all you need for great performance in daily tasks. The configurations available at writing time max out with 16GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, though you should see more options show up in the future. Past models have gone up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

Part of what makes this such a great laptop is the display, which is a 13.5-inch panel with a 3:2 aspect ratio. The base model comes with a Full HD+ IPS panel, which is already solid for the size. If you want an even better experience, there's also a 3K2K OLED model, which is sharper and has benefits like true blacks and more vibrant colors. Since it's a convertible, all models support touch and pen input. Every model also includes a 5MP webcam with Windows Hello support, one of the best you can find on any laptop.

HP hasn't changed the design of the Spectre x360 13.5 for 2023, so it's still a premium aluminum laptop with great build quality. It has a dual-tone design in both Nightfall Black or Nocturne Blue colorways, though there's also a simpler Natural Silver option. It weighs 3.01 pounds, which makes sense for a convertible of this size made of aluminum. For ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a microSD card reader, and a headphone jack.

Whether it's the performance, display, or design, there's a lot to love with the HP Spectre x360 13.5, and it's an easy recommendation for almost anyone. You can read our review of last year's model, which is very similar in many ways.

MacBook Air (M2)
Best premium laptop

Premium build and terrific performance

The latest MacBook Air is deceivingly lightweight and compact. Thanks to the Apple M2 processor, it's a surprisingly fast laptop with fantastic battery life, and it also has a great display with sharp resolution and accurate colors. Plus, it comes in four color options.

Pros
  • The Apple M2 processor offers fantastic performance and battery life
  • Sharp Retina display is included by default
  • 1080p webcam
  • One of the thinnest and most portable laptops
Cons
  • Only one external display is natively supported
  • Upgrades can get very expensive
  • The base storage configuration is noticeably slower
$1087 at Amazon (13 inches) $1099 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1099 at Adorama

No list of 13-inch laptops could go without mentioning the MacBook Air, and that's especially true with the latest model, which delivers some very welcome improvements in performance, design, and the webcam.

Focusing on performance first, the latest MacBook Air is powered by the Apple M2 with eight CPU cores, and it promises 18% more performance than the Apple M1. Plus, it also has an upgraded 10-core GPU (in some configurations), which delivers up to 35% more performance than the previous generation. This makes it one of the fastest lightweight laptops around, and it's also very efficient, so battery life is phenomenal. You can also configure the latest MacBook Air with up to 24GB of unified memory (RAM) and a 2TB SSD.

The display has also changed a bit in the latest MacBook Air. It's slightly taller and measures 13.6 inches diagonally, and the resolution is 2560x1664, so it's super sharp. It also now reaches up to 500 nits of brightness instead of 400 nits, so it's a bit more visible outdoors, and it supports P3 Wide Color. A potential downside is a notch that houses the webcam, but at least that webcam is now a 1080p sensor, so it has pretty good quality. It lacks Face ID support, though, so you still have to use Touch ID.

Design-wise, the latest MacBook Air is very different from its predecessors, ditching the wedge-shaped design for a flat, even look with rounded edges. It's still incredibly thin at just over 11mm, and it also weighs just 2.7 pounds. Plus, it comes in four colors, including the classic Silver and Space Grey, along with newcomers Starlight and Midnight. As for ports, you get two Thunderbolt/USB4 ports and a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, along with MagSafe 3 support for fast charging, a major addition to this model. It's worth noting that the laptop only supports one external display, however.

While it has some shortcomings, the latest MacBook Air is easily one of the best 13-inch laptops on the market today.

Dell XPS 13 Plus (2023)
Best clamshell laptop

Ultra-modern look with a fantastic display

$1249 $1499 Save $250

The Dell XPS 13 Plus is a powerful laptop with an ultra-modern design, featuring an invisible touchpad. It has powerful 13th-generation Intel processors and stunning display options, including a 3.5K OLED panel.

Pros
  • Powerful 13th-gen Intel P-series processors
  • Modern and futuristic design
  • Colorful and vibrant display, including an OLED model
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Only two Thunderbolt ports
  • Seamless trackpad and touch buttons may take some getting used to
$1249 at Dell $1950 at Best Buy $1692 at Amazon

Dell is another company well known for making some of the best laptops on the market, and the latest iteration of the XPS 13 Plus is yet another easy choice if you want a 13-inch laptop.

The latest model comes with 13th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically a Core i7-1360P with 12 cores and 16 threads, which gives it more than enough performance to handle all kinds of day-to-day tasks. This is a very fast processor, and it's combined with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

It's got a great display, too, being a 13.4-inch panel with a tall 16:10 aspect ratio. The base model comes in Full HD+ resolution, which is already great, but you can upgrade to a stunning 3.5K OLED option that's very sharp while also having the usual benefits of OLED, like true blacks, vivid colors, and high contrast ratios. There's also an Ultra HD+ LCD option, which is ideal for creative work since it covers 100% of Adobe RGB. The webcam is a big downside since Dell insists on using a 720p sensor. However, it does support Windows Hello, so there's a positive there.

A few things make this laptop a unique and appealing one in terms of design, too. It has an invisible touchpad that blends seamlessly with the chassis, and the zero-lattice keyboard combined with the touch-based function row all make for an incredibly modern-feeling laptop, though it may take some getting used to. It's also fairly light, at 2.71 pounds. A downside of this machine, however, is the ports, which are limited to just two Thunderbolt 4 connections, with adapters in the box for a headphone jack and USB Type-A.

The XPS 13 Plus certainly has its downsides, but it's still an incredible machine in more ways than one.

Microsoft Surface Laptop 5
Best premium Windows laptop

Sleek design and sharp display

$871 $999 Save $128

The Surface Laptop 5 is a premium laptop with top-tier specs and a great display for getting work done. It comes in an all-metal chassis that has four colors to choose from.

Pros
  • Tall 3:2 display is great for productivity
  • Premium build quality with an aluminum chassis and optional Alcantara fabric
  • Decent port selection including Thunderbolt 4
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • This design is a few years old at this point
$900 at Best Buy (13 inches) $871 at Amazon $871 at B&H

The Surface Laptop is kind of the Windows equivalent to the MacBook Air, and if you're looking for a premium laptop that runs Windows, then the Surface Laptop 5 is a safe bet.

For starters, the Surface Laptop 5 comes with 12th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically up to an Intel Core i7-1255U, with 10 cores and 12 threads. It's a very capable processor. It will handle day-to-day tasks just fine, though it's not the newest hardware around anymore. The 13-inch version of the Surface Laptop 5 goes up to 16GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD, so you have a great experience with this machine.

One of the highlights of the Surface Laptop 5 is definitely the display. Microsoft uses its signature 3:2 aspect ratio and a very sharp resolution of 2256x1504, so you'll have a great experience with it in every way. The screen also supports touch and pen input, though this isn't a convertible. A big downside to this laptop is the 720p webcam that sits at the top of the display, though it does support Windows Hello facial recognition.

Premium design is also a trademark of Surface devices, and the Surface Laptop 5 is no exception. You get an all-metal design in four color options, those being Platinum, Matte Black, Sandstone, and Sage. The Platinum model also comes with an Alcantara fabric cover for the keyboard base, making it a bit more comfortable. At 2.8 pounds, it's a fairly portable laptop, too. Ports include one Thunderbolt 4 port, one USB Type-A port, a headphone jack, and a Surface Connect port for charging. This is the first Surface laptop model with Thunderbolt support, which is a major addition.

The older processors and 720p webcam are notable downsides, but the Surface Laptop 5 is still a great premium laptop for most users. However, the Surface Laptop 6 could be right around the corner, so it may be worth waiting.

ASUS ROG Flow X13 (2023)
Best gaming laptop

Powerful performance in a lightweight package

The Asus ROG Flow X13 may be small, but it packs a punch. With an AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS and powerful Nvidia graphics, you can use this lightweight laptop for gaming while getting the versatility of a convertible. It has a beautiful display, too.

Pros
  • Powerful AMD processors and Nvidia graphics
  • Super lightweight and portable considering the performance
  • Beautiful Quad HD+ 165Hz display
Cons
  • Much more expensive than previous models
  • Battery life won't be great
$2500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070) $2500 at Asus $2900 at Newegg

Gaming usually requires pretty beefy hardware, but Asus managed to squeeze some gaming power into a portable machine with the ROG Flow X13, one of my favorite laptops ever.

The 2023 iteration of the ROG Flow X13 comes with an AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS processor with 8 cores and 16 threads, and that's paired with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU, delivering very impressive performance for such a small laptop. It also comes with up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD. If you want more graphics power, you can also hook up an Asus ROG Xg Mobile external GPU, with options up to an RTX 4090.

The display on the ROG Flow X13 is a 13.4-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, with options up to a stunning ROG Nebula display with Quad HD+ resolution, 100% coverage of DCI-P3, and a 165Hz refresh rate. It's fantastic for gaming and everything else. It supports touch and pen input, too. Above that display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support.

On to the design, the Asus ROG Flow X13 is very sleek with an all-black chassis that's just 18.7mm thick at its thickest point and a mere 2.87 pounds of weight, very impressive for these specs. It also has a solid supply of ports, with two USB Type-C ports (one integrated into the ROG Xg Mobile interface), USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. That's very good for this size.

This is a very impressive 13-inch laptop, and if you're interested in gaming while still having a lightweight design, it's one of your best options.

HP Dragonfly G4
Best business laptop

Sleek, lightweight, and performant

$1274 $2548 Save $1274

With top-of-the-line Intel processors, a sleek design, and a great webcam, the HP Dragonfly G4 is perfect for professionals, whether they're working from home or in the office. The lightweight chassis makes it easy to carry around, too, so it's great for travel.

Pros
  • Sleek and lightweight design makes it great for working from anywhere
  • Fast 13th-gen Intel processors
  • One of the best webcams on a laptop
Cons
  • Expensive, outside of sales
  • The non-OLED display isn't amazing
$1274 at HP $1769 at B&H $1752 at Newegg

Professional users need a reliable laptop that looks good in a business environment and is easy to carry around. The HP Dragonfly G4 ticks all these boxes, with some extra perks, too.

Inside the HP Dragonfly G4 are 13th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1365U with vPro, featuring 10 cores and 12 threads for great performance while having solid battery life. It also comes with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

You also get a 13.5-inch panel with a tall 3:2 aspect ratio here, and the base model comes in an average Full HD+ resolution with 400 nits of brightness. However, there are upgrade options including one with HP Sure View Reflect and 1000 nits of brightness, or a beautiful 3K2K OLED panel with vibrant colors. You even get a 5MP webcam, one of the best you'll find on any laptop, complete with Windows Hello support.

As for the design, it's as sleek and subdued as you could want. It comes in either Slate Blue or Natural Silver (though the former is more interesting), and it weighs just 2.2 pounds, meaning it's super portable and easy to carry. Plus, it has great connectivity options, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and an optional nano-SIM slot if you get the LTE or 5G models. And yes, cellular connectivity is an option if you want to work on the move.

Outside of discounts, the HP Dragonfly G4 is pretty pricy, but it's a fantastic laptop for almost everyone, and especially for business.

Microsoft Surface Pro 9
Best tablet

Extra portable and versatile

$954 $1000 Save $46

The Surface Pro 9 is arguably the best Windows tablet on the market, with top-tier performance and a fantastic display with a very sharp resolution and 120Hz refresh rate. It also comes in colors for the first time ever.

Pros
  • Very sharp 3:2 display with a smooth 120Hz refresh rate
  • Some of the best cameras on any Windows PC
  • Intel and Arm-based options, with the latter including 5G
Cons
  • Intel model no longer has the newest processors
  • Arm model doesn't support Thunderbolt
$1000 at Best Buy (Wi-Fi model) $1300 at Best Buy (5G model) $954 at Amazon

There aren't a lot of premium Windows tablets out there, but that shouldn't take away from how great the Surface Pro 9 is. With a great display and cameras, plus other high-end specs, this is a fantastic device.

On the inside, the Surface Pro 9 features 12th-generation Intel Core processors up to a Core i7-1255U. That gets you 10 cores and 12 threads, so it's plenty fast. You can also configure it with up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, which is fantastic for a tablet. There's also the Surface Pro 9 with 5G, which is powered by an Arm-based Microsoft SQ3 chipset, along with up to 16GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD. It's not as fast, but it's still great.

The display on the Surface Pro 9 is one of its best qualities. It has a 13-inch display with a 3:2 aspect ratio, and it comes in a very sharp 2880x1920 resolution and a smooth 120Hz refresh rate, which can adjust dynamically. Being a tablet also means the Surface Pro 9 has one of the best webcams around. The front-facing camera is a 5MP sensor that supports 1080p video, in addition to Windows Hello facial recognition. There's also a 10MP camera on the back with 4K video support.

Microsoft didn't change much about the design of the Surface Pro 9, so it still weighs around 1.94 pounds depending on the configuration, and it's also 9.4mm thin, excluding the optional keyboard. This model does add new color options in the Intel version, with Forest and Sapphire joining the Platinum and Graphite colorways. Unfortunately, in terms of ports, you only get two USB-C ports (supporting Thunderbolt 4, on the Intel model) and Surface Connect, meaning there's not even a headphone jack.

Despite that, the Surface Pro 9 is easily the best Windows tablet on the market, though we're expecting a Surface Pro 10 to launch very soon.

HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook
Best Chromebook

A premium ChromeOS convertible

The HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is possibly the most premium Chromebook on the market right now. It has a premium convertible design and high-end 12th-gen Intel processors, plus a fantastic display.

Pros
  • Fast 12th-gen Intel processors
  • Sharp 5MP webcam
  • Sharp and bright display
Cons
  • It's very expensive
  • No OLED display option like the Windows counterpart
$2177 at HP $1099 at Newegg

Not many companies are willing to put their best efforts into making a premium Chromebook, but HP has challenged that trend with the Elite Dragonfly Chromebook. This is one of the most premium Chromebooks around, despite being a year old.

For starters, in terms of performance, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook packs Intel's 12th-generation Core processors, up to an Intel Core i7-1265U, which has 10 cores and 12 threads and can run Chrome OS like a dream. Plus, you can get up to 32GB of RAM and a 512GB SSD, which is massive for ChromeOS.

The display on the Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is a 13.5-inch panel with a 3:2 aspect ratio. It comes in Full HD+ resolution in the base model, with the option to add HP Sure View Reflect, a privacy screen that prevents people around you from seeing what's on your screen. You can also upgrade to a "Quad HD+" model (2256x1504), and all models include a stylus, since the touchscreen also supports pen input. Above the display, you get a great 5MP webcam, but instead of facial recognition, this one has a fingerprint reader.

Design-wise, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is a fairly standard business laptop, coming in a subtle Slate Blue colorway that's very close to black. The laptop weighs 2.8 pounds, so it's fairly portable, especially considering it's a convertible. As for ports, the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook has two Thunderbolt 4 ports (though external GPUs won't work with Chrome OS), USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader.

The biggest downside of the HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook is its exorbitant price tag, but thankfully it gets discounted often. Otherwise, this is definitely one of the best 13-inch laptops on the market.

Lenovo Yoga 6 Gen 8
Best budget

A versatile laptop on the cheap

$555 $700 Save $145

The Lenovo Yoga 6 Gen 8 is an excellent budget Windows convertible, featuring Ryzen 7030 series processors and other solid specs, including a 1080p webcam and Windows Hello support.

Pros
  • Modern AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors
  • 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support
  • Unique design with optional fabric cover
Cons
  • Performance isn't on the same level as more expensive models
$700 at Best Buy $555 at Lenovo $555 at Newegg

Not everyone can afford to spend over $1,000 on a laptop, but thankfully, the Lenovo Yoga 6 to give you an excellent laptop for a very reasonable price. Starting at just $700, it's a no-brainer.

For that price, you're getting an AMD Ryzen 5 7530U, with 6 cores and 12 threads, delivering solid performance for all kinds of day-to-day tasks, whether that's browsing the web or writing up documents. It also has 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD, though you can upgrade both if you have the budget for it.

It also comes with a 13.3-inch display that has a 16:10 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution. That will give you a pretty great experience for the price, and the 300 nits of brightness are about what you might expect. It also supports touch and pen input, since it's a convertible. The webcam is a 1080p sensor, which is great to see in a budget laptop, and it even supports Windows Hello facial recognition.

Lenovo also has a fairly unique design here, with the top half of the laptop made of metal and the bottom half made of plastic. Some models also have a fabric cover on the top half, which makes it even more unique. Starting at 3.02 pounds, the laptop is also reasonably light. It even gets ports right, featuring two USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack to cover all your needs. There's no USB4 support, but that makes sense for a budget laptop.

Considering it's a convertible and everything else it offers, it's a very good deal. I reviewed the previous model, which was nearly identical aside from the processors, and praised it heavily.

Best 13-inch laptops in 2023: Bottom line

And those are the best 13-inch laptops you can buy today. We chose the HP Spectre x360 13.5 for the top spot because it has a fantastic combination of high-end performance, great display options, and a beautiful dual-tone design, plus the versatility of a convertible. It's a laptop you can't really go wrong with, so it deserves the top spot, but all the other laptops on this list are also great choices. A personal favorite of mine is the Asus ROG Flow X13 since it has the versatility of a convertible, but also some powerful internals that allow for some gaming.

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best overall
$900 $1500 Save $600

The Spectre x360 13.5 is one of the best convertibles HP has ever made, featuring a stunning premium design and a tall 3:2 display, all paired with high-end performance.

$900 at HP $1500 at Best Buy $1750 at Best Buy (OLED)

Of course, if you want something cheaper, the Lenovo Yoga 6 is the one for you. This is a personal favorite of mine, and it's simply a great laptop for the price, with solid performance, display, and design. You can't go wrong with this one if you're on a tighter budget.

If you're more interested in bigger laptops, we also have a list of the best 15-inch laptops you can buy right now. This larger size also makes room for more power, so you can get much more powerful gaming laptops.

item.title: What is Active Noise Cancelation?
item.itunes:title: What is Active Noise Cancelation?
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Active Noise Cancelation is a majorly sought-after feature on earphones and headphones, and for good reason. It removes a lot of outside noise from your listening experience, making it both significantly more peaceful and easier to listen at lower volumes. However, how noise-canceling works on headphones like the Sennheiser Momentum 4 Wireless and the Sony WH-1000XM5 is a bit of a mystery to many. We sat down with Christian Ern, a Principal Product Manager of Consumer Headphones at Sennheiser, at this year's IFA 2023 to help break it down.

Sound dampening is the first step to noise-canceling

First and foremost, the most important thing to understand about noise-canceling is that all of it has to do with frequencies, sound waves, and most of all, physics. Sounds travel in waves, which are made up of vibrations that pass through the air. The more frequent these vibrations are, the higher the frequency is (as the name suggests), and the height of the wave essentially determines the amplitude or loudness.

When dealing with higher-frequency sounds, these have a harder time penetrating surfaces, and this property goes the same for all types of frequencies. While sounds in content aimed at consumption by humans will typically never go past 20kHz (as that is the limit of human hearing range), mmWave 5G, for example, typically goes as high as 40GHz. At 40GHz, mmWave has trouble penetrating all kinds of surfaces, which is why it's a signal spectrum predominantly found outdoors in major cities.

Taking that understanding of the difficulty of high-frequency sounds in penetrating surfaces, this is why many high-end headphones with active noise-cancelation use thick, soft cups. While lower-frequency sounds can penetrate these with ease, higher-frequency sounds will be greatly filtered by a thick surface such as these. This technique is called sound dampening, and not active noise-cancelation.

Physics of destructive interference

When two coherent (to put it simply, similar) waves combine, this is called interference. If they combine to amplify the wave, then this is constructive interference, but if they cancel each other out, then this is called destructive interference. The concept of destructive interference is a huge piece of the active noise-cancelation puzzle.

Source: Wikipedia

Taking a look at the above image, note that the top line is a combination of the two waves below it. On the left, both signals are in phase, so they amplify and create a stronger wave as a result. On the right, an out-of-phase anti-wave results in destructive interference. In an audio context, one signal would be an outside sound wave, and the anti-wave generated to destroy it would be played through the headphones of the wearer listening to music.

If that's the case, then why do you still hear audio from outside of your headphones? Some sounds are removed completely, but there are never a perfect pair of noise-canceling headphones. Again, for similar reasons, it's all just about physics. This is where the microphones on the outside of your headphones come into play when it comes to noise-canceling and the difference between "feed-forward" and "feed-back" ANC.

Feedforward ANC versus feedback ANC

There are two different kinds of ANC in use in headphones, and Ern tells me that pretty much all flagship headphones will use a combination of both of these. Feedfoward will listen to audio outside of the headphones and try to cancel it out, whereas feedback ANC will have microphones inside the cups and listen for the difference between the inside of the headphones and the sound of what should be playing.

Feedforward ANC is particularly interesting, as it shows the specialized work that goes into creating noise-canceling headphones. In essence, a sound wave entering the ear cup will enter one of the microphones, and in the milliseconds it takes for that sound wave to reach your ear (as the microphone is a few centimeters away), it will try to process that sound wave and generate an anti-wave. For sounds facing the microphone, it works well in just milliseconds, but for sounds above and below it doesn't, as they don't directly enter the microphone and may reach the ear before they can be canceled out. That's why feedback ANC can close the gaps.

With feedback ANC, it uses microphones on the inside of the earcup to measure the difference between what should be playing versus what actually can be heard inside the earcup. When the sound from outside reaches this microphone, it's already being heard by the listener, but it can be used to remove constant noises, such as the hum of an airplane or other things. However, it can't help with sudden noises, which is why a combination of feedforward versus feedback is best.

As a side note, have you ever rubbed your fingers on the outside of your earphone or headphones and heard a high-pitched noise? Feedforward ANC is exactly why that happens, and high-end true wireless earphones with ANC are essentially the same setup as high-end headphones, just on a smaller scale.

Bringing it all together

When we combine feedforward and feedback active noise canceling, we get a powerful combination capable of canceling out lower-frequency sounds with great consistency. That's also why active noise-canceling headphones are great at filtering out the sound of things like public transport and other constant noises because they're predictable sounds that feedback ANC can successfully predict and remove. Combining that with the sound-dampening properties of thicker earcups that work well on higher frequencies, and you have all the technology that makes up the vast majority of active noise-canceling today.

Where this still falls apart is filtering out speech, and why active noise-canceling can struggle. The range of 1kHz to 3kHz is where most human speech resides, and this is just high enough to be harder for feedback ANC to cancel out but too directional to work well with feedforward ANC. Combining them introduces a lot of complexity (which is why typically mid-to-low-end headphones may only use one of these techniques and not both), and can also influence sound quality.

In this case, Ern tells me that headphones or earphones can be prioritized for sound quality or ANC, but not always both. The sound needs to be tuned for when ANC is on, but if you're removing frequencies that can be heard from outside, you run the risk of removing frequencies in your actual music, too. That's why noise-canceling is such a complicated feat of engineering, but the best ANC headphones all do very well at it.
